rulings.Case Title: ABDALLAH ELHANIA ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce This Courts December 10, 2024 Order And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 12, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC., AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE THIS COURTS DECEMBER 10, 2024 ORDER AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS is GRANTED, in part. Plaintiffs' refusal to agree to a HIPAA-qualified protective order violates this Court's December 10, 2024 Order and undermines the process this Court's Order established for Airbnb to obtain Decedent's medical records from third-party providers. Given Plaintiffs' disregard of the Order's directive and its intended purpose, the Court grants Defendant's request to modify the Court's protective order entered on January 14, 2025 to satisfy HIPAA's requirements. Although the Court's finds sanctions are warranted under Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.010, Defendant failed to provide the Court with any information aside from the total attorney hours spent on the motion. Accordingly, sanctions are denied, without prejudice pending a supplemental declaration with the necessary information to support sanctions. Declaration to be filed no later than May 12, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. Plaintiffs' request to continue the hearing on this motion until after the Court hears Plaintiffs' Motion to Disqualify is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PETER LEE ET AL VS. 915 NORTH POINT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 12, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF PETER LEE, MONICA LEE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to show the likelihood of success on the merits or irreparable harm. The Court does not find at this time, based on the evidence submitted in support of the motion, that Defendants had actual or constructive notice of the settlement agreement containing the restrictive covenants. There is no admissible evidence demonstrating that Jamestown L.P. disclosed to Defendants the discussions and/or agreement it had with Plaintiffs, or that it provided the settlement agreement to Defendants prior to the purchase. Further, based on the evidence submitted, the court cannot conclude one way or the other, that Exhibit G, attached to the Purchase and Sales Agreement, put Defendants on "inquiry" notice. As to the harm, the noise concern is speculative as the HVAC units are not yet operable. Likewise, Plaintiffs can only speculate as to how Defendants will market the property and Defendants claim they intend to follow the law. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. JAIME AGUILAR MALDONADO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 12, 2025 line 7. DEFENDANT JAIME MALDONADO MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of personal service on defendant on file as of five days before the hearing. The proof of personal service filed subsequently states that the moving papers were personally served on defendant on a date that was three days before the papers were signed, which is impossible. The documents served to defendant must be identical to the documents filed with the court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
